skill.name: gws-shared
skill.description: Shared reference patterns for using the gws command-line tool with Google Workspace authentication, flags, output formats, and shell quoting.
skill.summary: Use consistent authentication, formatting, and safety patterns with the gws Google Workspace CLI.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many command, network, credential, weak-crypto, and obfuscation patterns. Manual review found a documentation-only shared reference with gws CLI examples, GitHub links, and a service-account environment variable example, not executable code or confirmed malicious behavior. Publish risk is low, with user caution needed around running CLI commands and handling Google credentials.

low_findings.title: CLI Command Examples Require User Control
low_findings.description: The external command findings are true as documentation patterns but false positives for embedded code execution. The file contains examples for installing and invoking the gws CLI, including authentication and request examples, so users should approve commands and inspect parameters before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible markdown examples and command syntax, not scripts bundled for automatic execution. Risk remains low because agents may still execute these examples if a user asks them to.

low_findings.title: Credential Environment Variable Documented
low_findings.description: The env_access finding is a true documentation pattern but not secret theft. The skill shows how to set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS for a service account and also instructs users not to output secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact environment variable example is present, and there is no evidence that the value is read or transmitted by skill code. The main risk is accidental credential exposure during CLI use.

low_findings.title: Repository Links Are Benign Network References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are true but low risk. They point to the public project repository and issue tracker for feedback, with no evidence of data exfiltration or hidden network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are visible GitHub references used for community feedback. No code in the reviewed file sends credentials or user data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Obfuscation Findings Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm and dangerous-combination findings are false positives after manual review. No evidence found for cryptographic operations, obfuscated payloads, prompt injection, or malicious combined behavior in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced locations contain descriptive text, flag tables, and safety advice. They do not show hashing, encryption, obfuscation, or instructions to bypass the audit.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill teaches use of the local gws binary. This is expected for the skill, but marketplace users should understand that commands may call Google Workspace APIs when run.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI syntax is explicit and central to the skill. It is a controlled documentation pattern, not hidden code execution.

content.user_title: Manage gws CLI Patterns for Workspace
content.value_statement: Google Workspace CLI work can fail when authentication, flags, and shell quoting are inconsistent. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a shared reference for safer gws commands.
actual_capabilities: Explains browser-based OAuth and service account authentication patterns for gws.
Documents global output formats including json, table, yaml, and csv.
Summarizes shared method flags for parameters, request bodies, uploads, pagination, and binary output.
Provides safety guidance for write and delete commands, dry runs, and response sanitization.
Gives shell quoting guidance for sheet ranges and structured command arguments.
limitations: Does not install the gws binary or verify it is available on the local PATH.
Does not authenticate users or create Google Cloud service account keys.
Does not replace product-specific Google Workspace command documentation.
Cannot guarantee API permissions, quotas, or organization policy settings.

use_cases.title: Prepare Workspace CLI Sessions
use_cases.description: Set up consistent authentication and global flags before running Google Workspace commands.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Command Output
use_cases.description: Choose predictable formats and pagination settings for reports, exports, and troubleshooting output.

use_cases.title: Avoid Shell Quoting Errors
use_cases.description: Use safer quoting patterns for sheet ranges and structured arguments in terminal commands.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Authentication
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new gws session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the right gws authentication method for my Google Workspace task. Ask before suggesting any command that writes data.

prompt_templates.title: Format A Read Command
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a read-only command with predictable output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a read-only gws command for my Workspace task using a clear output format and safe pagination settings.

prompt_templates.title: Review Command Safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this gws command for authentication, write risk, output handling, and shell quoting issues before I run it.

prompt_templates.title: Plan A Multi-Step Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-step gws workflow for my Workspace operation. Include dry-run checks, confirmation points, output formats, and rollback notes.

output_examples.input: User asks how to authenticate for a Workspace automation task.
output_examples.output: A short comparison of OAuth and service account options, with the safer choice tied to the task context.

output_examples.input: User asks why a Sheets range fails in the terminal.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis of shell history expansion risk and a corrected quoting approach for the range.

output_examples.input: User asks for a report command with stable output.
output_examples.output: A read-only command plan that selects an output format, pagination limit, and review step before execution.

best_practices: Confirm with the user before running any command that writes, deletes, uploads, or changes permissions.
Use dry-run and sanitization options when available for sensitive or destructive operations.
Keep credentials out of command output, logs, screenshots, and shared transcripts.
anti_patterns: Do not print API keys, OAuth tokens, or service account key contents in responses.
Do not assume a write command is safe because it has a familiar service name.
Do not ignore shell quoting when commands include punctuation, ranges, or structured arguments.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It provides shared guidance for authentication, global flags, output formats, and shell quoting when using the gws CLI.

faq.question: Does it install gws?
faq.answer: No. It assumes the gws binary is already installed and available on the local PATH.

faq.question: Can it run write commands automatically?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to confirm with the user before executing write or delete commands.

faq.question: Does it handle service account credentials?
faq.answer: It documents the credential environment variable, but users must protect key files and avoid exposing secrets.

faq.question: Which output formats are covered?
faq.answer: The reference covers json, table, yaml, and csv output formats for gws commands.

faq.question: Is this product-specific documentation?
faq.answer: No. It is a shared reference for common gws patterns across Google Workspace services.
